title: Near-miss: a long backchannel that briefly looks like a floor-take (should NOT yield)

transcript.agent: And then the technician will call you when they are about fifteen minutes out so you have time to move the car out of the driveway before they arrive.
transcript.caller: yeah no totally that makes sense

why_it_matters: This backchannel is long enough and energetic enough that a pure energy or duration threshold could mistake it for a genuine floor-take, yet it is still just agreement, not a bid to take over. It is the deliberate near-miss: the hardest hold in the set. A correct agent still holds. Its bad twin lives in funnel-demo (fd-02), where an agent yields to almost exactly this kind of backchannel and fails. That contrast is the whole point: one sensitivity dial cannot both hold here and catch a real interruption elsewhere.
